No Poverty.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (4.3), develops language skills for work/life (4.4), and fosters cultural understanding and humanistic literacy (4.7), directly aligning with multiple targets.

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to equip students with language skills for 'work' and 'professional competitiveness,' directly contributing to productive employment (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course fosters an 'international perspective' and 'international mobility,' which can indirectly promote intercultural understanding and social inclusion (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's focus on understanding Japanese culture and customs can be a minor, indirect contribution to appreciating and safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to responsible consumption or production.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to climate change or action.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Fostering an 'international perspective' and understanding other cultures promotes intercultural dialogue, indirectly contributing to peace and inclusive societies (Target 16.b).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Developing language skills, an 'international perspective,' and 'international mobility' are enabling competencies for international cooperation and knowledge sharing (Targets 17.6, 17.9).
